skill.name: golang-pro
skill.description: Master Go 1.21+ with modern patterns, advanced concurrency, performance optimization, and production-ready microservices. Expert in the latest Go ecosystem including generics, workspaces, and cutting-edge frameworks. Use PROACTIVELY for Go development, architecture design, or performance optimization.
skill.summary: Develop production-ready Go services with modern concurrency, testing, architecture, and performance practices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography and network reconnaissance patterns, but review found descriptive Go development text only. No executable code, external network calls, credential handling, or prompt injection attempts were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm detections occur in prose describing Go development, architecture, APIs, and examples. No actual cryptographic implementation, weak hash selection, encryption call, or instruction to use unsafe algorithms is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are descriptive markdown and contain no code paths, algorithms, or configuration values. Context confirms the lines discuss Go topics rather than cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Alert Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance detection occurs on a behavioral guidance line about simplicity and readability. No scanner, port enumeration, host probing, network command, or instruction to discover targets appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is ordinary prose about code style. There is no adjacent networking context or executable content that could support a reconnaissance interpretation.

content.value_statement: Go teams need reliable guidance for concurrency, architecture, performance, and production readiness. This skill provides focused Go 1.21+ support for services, APIs, tooling, tests, and optimization.
actual_capabilities: Design Go services, CLIs, APIs, and microservices using Go 1.21+ practices.
Recommend concurrency patterns such as worker pools, pipelines, channels, and graceful shutdown.
Guide profiling, benchmarking, memory tuning, and performance optimization workflows.
Advise on testing strategies, table-driven tests, benchmarks, mocks, and integration tests.
Support Go architecture decisions including clean architecture, interfaces, dependency injection, and modules.
Suggest production practices for observability, containers, Kubernetes, CI/CD, and secure coding.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, but it does not run Go tooling by itself.
It depends on the host assistant for repository access, builds, tests, and profiling.
It is focused on Go and may not fit projects driven by another runtime.
It does not replace security review for production cryptography or authentication designs.

use_cases.title: Plan a Go Service
use_cases.description: Design service boundaries, API structure, module layout, error handling, testing, and deployment strategy before implementation.

use_cases.title: Improve Concurrent Code
use_cases.description: Review goroutine lifecycles, channel usage, cancellation, synchronization, and race-condition risks in a Go codebase.

use_cases.title: Optimize Go Performance
use_cases.description: Use benchmarks, pprof, traces, allocation analysis, and targeted refactors to improve latency, throughput, or memory use.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Go Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing Go package
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Go package and explain its structure, main responsibilities, and any simple improvements for readability and idiomatic Go.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Go 1.21+ service for this feature. Include package layout, API boundaries, error handling, tests, observability, and deployment notes.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Concurrency Risks
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this concurrent Go code for goroutine leaks, race conditions, cancellation gaps, channel misuse, and shutdown problems. Recommend specific fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Production Path
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the provided benchmarks, profiles, and code context to identify performance bottlenecks. Propose prioritized Go changes with validation steps.

output_examples.input: Design a worker pool with graceful shutdown.
output_examples.output: A structured design covering worker lifecycle, context cancellation, bounded queues, error propagation, shutdown order, tests, and observability points.

output_examples.input: Review this API package for production readiness.
output_examples.output: A review summarizing routing, request validation, error responses, logging, metrics, authentication boundaries, test gaps, and deployment concerns.

output_examples.input: Optimize memory use in this Go service.
output_examples.output: A performance plan that separates measurement, allocation hotspots, pooling tradeoffs, garbage collection effects, benchmark design, and regression checks.

best_practices: Provide Go version, module layout, runtime constraints, and target deployment environment.
Include relevant tests, benchmarks, profiles, logs, and production symptoms when asking for optimization help.
Ask for validation steps so proposed changes can be checked with Go tests, race detection, and benchmarks.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad rewrites without service goals, constraints, or expected behavior.
Do not optimize Go code before measuring the bottleneck with profiles or benchmarks.
Do not use this skill for unrelated languages or generic cloud architecture without Go implementation needs.

faq.question: Does this skill write Go code?
faq.answer: It can guide an assistant to write or review Go code when repository context is available.

faq.question: Which Go versions does it target?
faq.answer: It targets modern Go 1.21+ practices, while still considering project-specific version constraints.

faq.question: Can it help with concurrency bugs?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on goroutine lifecycles, channels, synchronization, cancellation, and race-condition prevention.

faq.question: Can it replace performance profiling?
faq.answer: No. It helps interpret benchmarks and profiles, but real measurements are still required.

faq.question: Is it useful for microservices?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers APIs, gRPC, observability, deployment, testing, and production readiness for Go services.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes secure coding topics, but specialized cryptography and authentication designs still need expert review.
